problem_statement: Flight search by destination airport returns no results because the destination filter in flight search likely performs a case-sensitive comparison against uppercase-stored airport codes, so a request like `destination=jfk` does not match flights stored as `JFK`.
goal: Make `/flights/search` return flights when the destination airport code matches case-insensitively, normalizing the supplied destination code before filtering or comparing in a case-insensitive way.
acceptance_criteria: GET /flights/search?destination=jfk returns flights whose destination airport code is stored as JFK.
Destination filtering works regardless of input casing (e.g. jfk, Jfk, JFK).
Existing search behavior for other filters remains unchanged.
constraints: Keep the fix localized to the flight search implementation if possible.
Airport codes are stored in uppercase, so comparisons should normalize input rather than changing stored data.
unknowns: Whether the search logic also handles other airport-related filters that may have the same case-sensitivity issue.
Whether the destination parameter is normalized at the API route layer or only inside the service method.
repo_summary: Relevant backend code is concentrated under `backend/app/services/flight_service.py`, with route wiring in `backend/app/api/routes/flights.py` and tests in `backend/app/tests/test_flight_service.py`.

targeted_symbols.reason: Likely contains the destination filter logic that compares the query parameter against stored airport codes; normalize casing here to fix the empty-result bug.
Add or adjust a test to verify destination search is case-insensitive and returns matching flights for lowercase input.

localization_rationale: The issue explicitly names `backend/app/services/flight_service.py (search_flights)` as the affected file.
The symptom is specific to destination filtering, not general flight retrieval or routing, which points to the service-layer search predicate.
The minimal safe fix is likely a normalization step on the destination query value before matching against uppercase airport codes.
A corresponding service test is the smallest verification point to prevent regression.
